front: Painting: nude models ('living brushes') covered in blue paint roll and press on canvases to create imprints
Painting: 20-minute making-of film accompanied by a single D-flat chord called the 'Monotone Silence Symphony'
Painting: recreated in the film Mondo Cane, the night of which the artist had a fatal heart attack
Painting: Rachel Lachowicz appropriated this series using lipstick instead of paint
Other: empty white gallery with only an empty display cabinet at the Iris Clert Gallery
Other: 3,000 visitors served blue cocktails at the door
Other: Arman filled the Iris Clert Gallery with trash in response (Le Plein / The Full-Up)
Other: opening marked by the release of 1,001 balloons in trademark blue
Other: Brian O'Doherty's 'white cube' essays discuss how the gallery was presented by itself in this exhibition
Other: exhibition consisting of only the color white (not blue), despite the artist's trademark color
Photograph: doctored photo by Harry Shunk and Jean Kender showing artist apparently jumping from a second-story rooftop
Photograph: collected in the artbook Dimanche
Photograph: artist wears a black suit with a bicyclist visible in the background
Painting: People Begin to Fly depicts blue silhouettes echoing a photograph of the artist leaping from a building
Painting: eleven identical, differently priced blue canvases at the Proposte monocrome exhibition at the Galleria Apollinaire in Milan
Painting: eleven identical blue canvases inspired Piero Manzoni's white Achromes
Painting: Derek Jarman's experimental 1993 AIDS film Blue approximated this color
Painting: applied IKB to headless plaster casts of Venus de Milo
Painting: manifesto of Nouveau Realisme was signed over this color, applied to many spongy canvases
Other: sold certificates of ownership of non-existent spaces for gold
back: Zone of Immaterial Pictorial Sensibility (Yves Klein)

front: Other: threw gold dust into the Seine and burned the receipt
back: Zone of Immaterial Pictorial Sensibility (Yves Klein)

front: Painting: burned asbestos-covered cardboard with a blowtorch to make 'fire paintings'
Painting: Cosmogonies series created by fastening canvases to his Citroen car to expose to rain, wind, and dirt
Artist: Monogold series consisted of panels using gold with the 'mono-' prefix
Artist: Monotone Symphony is a single long chord followed by silence, used as background for exhibitions and performances
Artist: co-founded Nouveau Realisme with critic Pierre Restany
Artist: Nouveau Realisme described as French manifestation of Pop Art
Artist: avid judo practitioner and instructor
Artist: Chelsea Hotel Manifesto theorized the void and states 'birds must be eliminated'
Artist: fascination with Rosicrucianism led to obsession with emptiness and the void
Artist: Pierre Restany coined the term Nouveau Realisme and edited art magazine Domus
Artist: Nouveau Realisme manifesto signed in his apartment, written in white cursive on blue canvas, advocating return to reality
Artist: Christo and Jeanne-Claude, Jean Tinguely, and Niki de Saint-Phalle were fellow members of this artist's movement
